data.title: Kelly announces selection of Chris Layden as CEO
data.description:
• Kelly (NASDAQ:KELYA) on Thursday appointed Chris Layden as president and chief executive officer, effective September 2.
  • Layden will succeed Peter Quigley, who announced his intent to retire as president and CEO.
  • Quigley will remain as a strategic advisor to Kelly to ensure a smooth transition and will continue to serve as a member of the board of directors until the company’s next Annual Shareholders Meeting in May 2026.
  • Most recently, Layden served as COO of Prolink. Before joining Prolink, Layden spent nearly two decades at ManpowerGroup, serving in a range of senior roles spanning general management, regional leadership, corporate strategy, and sales.
  • KELYA +2.04% premarket to $12.5.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Belite Bio announces registered direct offering of $15 million
data.description:
• Belite Bio (NASDAQ:BLTE) on Thursday said it has entered into a securities purchase agreement for the purchase and sale of 230,770 American Depositary Shares and warrants to purchase 230,770 ADSs, at a purchase price of $65 per ADS and accompanying warrant, in a registered direct offering.
  • The offering is expected to result in gross proceeds of approximately $15 million.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about August 8.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • BLTE +1% premarket to $68.67.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DigitalBridge posts Q2 distributable loss on loss from fund investment
data.description:
DigitalBridge (NYSE:DBRG) stock slid 1.4% in Thursday premarket trading after the asset manager that invests in digital infrastructure reported Q2 earnings that were hit by a realized loss from a fund investment.

Q2 after-tax distributable EPS of -$0.10, vs. the average analyst estimate of $0.05, fell from $0.29 in Q1 and $0.11 in last year's Q2. The current quarter included a $40M realized loss from an InfraBridge fund investment, resulting in the distributable loss for the quarter.

Fee-related earnings of $32.0M slipped from $35.0M in the previous quarter and increased from $26.0M in the year-ago period. 

Q2 revenue of -$3.21M, trailing the $101.6M consensus, sank from $45.4M in the previous quarter and $390.3M a year ago. A reversal of carried interest allocation subtracted $115.1M from Q2 2025 revenue, compared with a reversal of $55.5M in Q1 and an allocation of $288.2M in Q2 2024.

Fee revenue of $85.3M vs. $90.1M in Q1 and $78.6M in Q2 2024.

Fee-earning equity under management of $39.7B vs. $37.3B at the end of Q1 and up 21% Y/Y.

Expenses of $32.4M dropped from $55.3M in the prior quarter and $288.5M a year ago.

Earlier, DigitalBridge Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.10, revenue of $3.21M misses by $104.78M

data.title: Archer Aviation makes two acquisitions as it shifts the business more toward military applications
data.description:
Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) announced two strategic acquisitions on Thursday aimed at accelerating its defense program in response to growing demand for advanced vertical takeoff and landing capabilities, particularly within the U.S. Department of Defense. 

One of the acquisitions is of a patent portfolio and hiring of key talent from Overair, a spin-off of Karem Aircraft. The other is the acquisition of key composite manufacturing assets and a ~60,000 square foot manufacturing facility from Mission-Critical Composites, a specialized defense composite manufacturer in Southern California.

Financial terms of the deals were not disclosed.

The company, which was originally focused on building electric air taxis for urban mobility, noted that it has shifted substantial resources toward military applications as geopolitical tensions rise and U.S. defense strategy prioritizes rapid adoption of new technologies such as AI, drones, and hybrid aircraft.

"The Administration has made it clear: Leading in advanced aviation in both commercial and defense is a national priority. These acquisitions are part of our commitment—we’re working to accelerate our product development to meet our country’s needs," highlighted Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) CEO Adam Goldstein,

Shares of Archer Aviation (ACHR) rose 2.5% in premarket action on Thursday. The eVTOL stock is only up 1.5% on a year-to-date basis. Short interest on ACHR stands at 13.9% of the total float.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: TSM, INTC, TM, FTNT
data.description:
Stock index futures gained on Thursday, as traders weighed President Donald Trump's latest chip tariffs, softened by sweeping exemptions. Here are four stocks to watch on Thursday:

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) shares were up 4.5% in premarket trade after Taiwan said its top chip manufacturer will not have to pay a 100% tariff on such imports to the US. This comes after President Donald Trump said he plans to impose a 100% tariff on semiconductor imports. An exemption for companies that manufacture or commit to manufacturing in the US.

Intel (INTC) stock fell 2% in extended trading after President Donald Trump called for the resignation of the chipmaker's new CEO, Lip-Bu Tan. Trump's remarks come on the heels of a letter sent by U.S. Republican Senator Tom Cotton to Intel's board chair asking questions about Tan's relations with Chinese companies. He also mentioned a recent criminal case involving his former company, Cadence Design Systems.

Toyota Motor (TM) shares were down 1.3% in premarket trade after the firm said it expects a $9.5 billion hit from U.S. levies that have rattled the global automotive industry. The world’s biggest carmaker now sees ¥3.2 trillion in operating income for the fiscal year ending in March 2026, down from its initial forecast of ¥3.8 trillion.

Fortinet (FTNT) shares tumbled 21% before the market open after it reported second quarter 2025 financial results featuring stronger-than-expected billings revenue but in-line revenue. The company expects Q4 adjusted EPS ranging from $0.62 to $0.64, with the midpoint being a hair above the $0.62 consensus. The company projects revenue to range from $1.67B to $1.73B, with a midpoint of $1.7B, just below the $1.71B estimate.
